course_description:
This course allows students to undertake an internship and related work-integrated learning, or a research project on a topic decided in consultation with the course coordinator.

This course consists of an industry placement in the field of writing, editing, and publishing, or communication. Students will undertake 80-100 hours of work-integrated learning, "solve" a problem for their provider, and write a reflective essay.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
At least 4 units of WEP courses
incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

timetable:
Additional timetable information
There are no formal classes for this course.
course_aims: To enable students to undertake and reflect on a work-integrated learning experience.

learning_outcomes.description: Structure and carry out workplace tasks thoughtfully and intelligently.
Develop and express a critical and reflexive perspective on work tasks.

assessment_summary.title: Identify a problem in the workplace
assessment_summary.due_date: 26/08/2024 4:00 pm; Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard

assessment_summary.title: Reflective Work Journal
assessment_summary.due_date: 4/11/2024 4:00 pm; Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard

assessment_details.title: Identify a problem in the workplace
assessment_details.due_date: 26/08/2024 4:00 pm Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Identify an opportunity for improvement in the workplace where you are undertaking your internship. It could be a system that could be streamlined, a lack of documentation in some aspect of work flow, or an idea for software or equipment that would make things easier for management or employees. Write up a 500-word proposal: Paragraph 1: a concise statement of opportunity (9 marks) Paragraph 2: your ideas for advancing it (15 marks) Paragraph 3: any resourcing issues applicable to your solution (6 marks) Feel free to talk through with your internship provider, but please make sure they know this is a hypothetical only. If they want you to implement the solution, they will let you know. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using TurnItIn . Before submitting any assignments for this course you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
When you successfully submit your assessment to TurnItIn you will see text confirming your submission is complete, before being redirected to your Assignment inbox. On this page you can:
View the name of the submitted file View date and time of the upload Resubmit your paper (if necessary) Download your submitted paper Download digital receipt.
If you cannot see your submission in your Assignment inbox you should regard your submission as unsuccessful. Students are responsible for retaining evidence of submission by the due date for all assessment items, in the required form (e.g. screenshot, email, photo, and an unaltered copy of submitted work).
If the submission was not successful:
Note the error message (preferably take a screenshot). Go to your assignment page and see if it is possible to submit again. If you cannot submit again email your course coordinator immediately with the assignment attached.
Please visit this webpage for further advice on how to submit your TurnItIn assignment .
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Reflective Work Journal
assessment_details.due_date: 4/11/2024 4:00 pm Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The student will submit a reflective journal of 3,500 words, based on experiences and observations during their internship. The journal may include up to 1500 words of written material, if such written material was composed for the internship provider, for example, media releases, blog posts, editorial reports. If there is a commercial-in-confidence requirement at your workplace, you will need to de-identify these before submission. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (22 Jul - 27 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: 80-100 hours of work-integrated learning via placement Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments - Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Learning activities
